steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a single curving line for the frog's back — a low arch that rises from the front of the head, peaks above the hips, and dips toward the rear. This is the line of action.

steps.title: Block the big shapes (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a rounded shape for the head at one end of the gesture and a larger rounded shape for the body at the other. They overlap slightly — the frog is one continuous form, not two beads.

steps.title: Add the legs (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch the folded back legs as two zigzag shapes tucked along the body — thigh, calf, foot. The front legs are shorter and prop the chest up; sketch them as simple posts under the head.

steps.title: Place the eyes (pencil)
steps.description: On top of the head, sketch two small bumps for the eyes — frogs' eyes sit on top, not on the sides. They should be close together and roughly mirrored.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace the frog's outer outline in one flowing pass — head, over the eye bumps, along the back, around the haunches, under the belly. Where the back legs meet the body, land those lines cleanly at the junction.

steps.title: Ink the legs and feet (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, define the leg folds with a short curve where thigh meets calf. Add three or four toes on each foot — short lines with tiny pads at the tips.

steps.title: Ink the face (ink)
steps.description: Trace the eye bumps as small circles, with a dot inside each for the pupil. Add a wide curving line for the mouth, running from below one eye to below the other. A small nostril dot on each side finishes the face.

steps.title: Suggest texture (ink)
steps.description: Scatter a few small dots or short marks across the back to hint at frog skin. A handful is enough — keep the belly area quiet so the textured back reads against it.
